tasks.title: Project Setup and Directory Structure
tasks.description: Initialize Python 3.11 project with uv/pip, create directory layout, requirements.txt, and basic config.py with paths and presets registry.
tasks.details: Use `uv init musicvids`; create folders: pipeline/, presets/, assets/shaders/, assets/fonts/, cache/, outputs/; generate requirements.txt with gradio==4.x, torch==2.x (cuda12), diffusers, librosa, soundfile, moderngl, skia-python, pillow, numpy; config.py with default paths, preset dict loading YAMLs.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify dir structure exists, `uv sync` installs without errors, `python config.py` loads without ImportError.
tasks.expansionPrompt: No expansion needed; straightforward setup.

tasks.title: Gradio UI Skeleton Implementation
tasks.description: Build single-page Gradio layout with all input sections: Audio, Metadata, Branding, Lyrics, Visual style, Output settings, Actions buttons.
tasks.details: In app.py: gr.Interface or Blocks with Tabs/Rows for sections; file uploads for audio/logo; Textboxes for metadata/lyrics/prompt; Dropdown for presets; Sliders for opacity/intensity; Buttons for Analyze/Preview/Render; Progress panel with gr.Progress.
tasks.testStrategy: Launch `python app.py`, verify all UI elements render, uploads/inputs accept data, buttons clickable without crash.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) Define Gradio Blocks layout and tabs; 2) Implement input components (uploads, textboxes, sliders, dropdown); 3) Add action buttons and progress panel with event handlers.

tasks.title: Audio Loading and Waveform Preview
tasks.description: Implement audio file upload handling, 44.1kHz mono mixdown for analysis, stereo original preservation, waveform preview/play/scrub in Gradio.
tasks.details: Use soundfile/librosa to load MP3/WAV/FLAC; resample to 44100Hz mono; generate numpy waveform; Gradio Audio component for preview, custom JS for scrub if needed; hash song for cache dir.
tasks.testStrategy: Upload sample audio, verify waveform displays, play/scrub works, cache/<hash>/original.wav saved.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Audio loading, resampling, and caching with hashing; 2) Waveform generation and Gradio Audio component integration with preview/playback.

tasks.title: Audio Analyzer with BeatNet and Features
tasks.description: Implement Analyze button: tempo/beats (BeatNet/madmom), onsets (librosa), spectral bands (8 log-mel), RMS, structural segments; cache analysis.json.
tasks.details: pipeline/audio_analyzer.py: load vocals via demucs('htdemucs_ft'); BeatNet for beats; librosa.onset.onset_strength.peaks; librosa.feature.melspectrogram(8 bands); librosa.rms; librosa.segment; save json with beats/onsets/spectrum/rms/segments.
tasks.testStrategy: Run Analyze on sample, verify analysis.json fields populated accurately vs librosa docs, BPM matches known tracks.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Demucs vocal separation; 2) Beat/tempo detection with BeatNet/madmom; 3) Onset, spectral, RMS feature extraction with librosa; 4) Structural segmentation and JSON caching.

tasks.title: Orchestrator Core with Song Hashing and Caching
tasks.description: Create orchestrator.py to coordinate pipeline stages, compute song_hash from audio+metadata, manage cache/<hash>/, skip re-analysis if exists.
tasks.details: Hash = hashlib.sha256(audio_bytes + metadata_str); mkdir cache/<hash>; load/save analysis.json/stems; orchestrate stages sequentially with progress callbacks to Gradio.
tasks.testStrategy: Simulate re-run with existing cache, verify skips analysis, progress updates correctly.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down to: 1) Implement song hashing from audio/metadata; 2) Cache directory management and JSON/stems persistence; 3) Sequential pipeline orchestration with Gradio progress callbacks and skip logic.

tasks.title: Preset System and YAML Loading
tasks.description: Define presets/neon-synthwave.yaml etc. with SD prompt/shader/typography/color; load in config.py; populate Gradio dropdown.
tasks.details: YAML schema: {prompt: str, shader: str, typo_style: str, colors: list}; config.py registry[list]; Gradio dropdown onchange loads fields; create 6 presets as specified.
tasks.testStrategy: Select preset, verify fields auto-fill (prompt/shader), yaml parse without error.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) YAML schema definition, 6 preset files creation, and config.py registry; 2) Gradio dropdown integration with auto-fill onchange.

tasks.title: Simple Spectrum Visualizer Renderer (M1)
tasks.description: For M1: render plain 1080p video with solid color bg + spectrum overlay from analysis, pipe raw BGR to ffmpeg NVENC, mux audio.
tasks.details: pipeline/renderer.py: loop over frames at 30fps, interpolate spectrum[frame], draw bars with numpy/Pillow; ffmpeg cmd as spec'd: rawvideo bgr24 stdin -> h264_nvenc cq19; test end-to-end.
tasks.testStrategy: Render full song, ffprobe output.mp4: 1080p30, no A/V drift >50ms, spectrum syncs to beats.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Decompose into: 1) Frame loop with spectrum interpolation; 2) Numpy/Pillow bar drawing on solid BG; 3) Raw BGR piping to ffmpeg NVENC; 4) Audio muxing and A/V sync validation.

tasks.title: Moderngl Reactive Shader Layer
tasks.description: Implement GPU shader pass with moderngl offscreen FBO for reactive overlays driven by beat_phase, band_energies[8], rms, onset_pulse.
tasks.details: assets/shaders/: glsl files for spectrum/particles/geometry (e.g. #version 330; uniforms vec4 band_energies; frag: color based on sin(time)*rms); moderngl.Context offscreen 1920x1080 RGBA; render_frame(uniforms_dict) -> np.array alpha.
tasks.testStrategy: Render 10s clip, verify shader responds to mock audio features (visual pulses on beats), no GL errors.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) GLSL shader files for spectrum/particles/geometry; 2) Moderngl Context/FBO offscreen setup; 3) Uniforms integration (beat_phase, bands, rms); 4) Frame rendering to numpy RGBA; 5) GL error handling and testing.

tasks.title: Preset Shader Selection and Reactive Intensity
tasks.description: Integrate presets to select shader, apply intensity slider (0-100%) scaling uniforms, composite reactive RGBA onto bg.
tasks.details: In reactive_layer.py: load preset.shader glsl; uniforms['intensity'] = slider/100; gl.teximage background_rgb; render -> alpha_blend.
tasks.testStrategy: Vary intensity 0/50/100, verify visual strength scales linearly in output frames.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Preset-based GLSL loading and uniforms scaling by intensity slider; 2) Background texture compositing and alpha blending.

tasks.title: Logo Compositing
tasks.description: Load optional logo PNG, apply position (corners/center), opacity slider, blend RGBA fixed per frame.
tasks.details: Pillow: Image.open(logo).resize; position mapping dict; alpha = opacity/100; np blend logo_rgba onto frame.
tasks.testStrategy: Upload logo, set pos/opacity, render frame extract verifies positioned/transparent correctly.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) PNG loading/resizing/positioning with Pillow; 2) Opacity blending onto frames with numpy.

tasks.title: Lyrics Alignment with WhisperX
tasks.description: Implement lyrics_aligner.py: demucs vocals, WhisperX large-v3 word timestamps, Needleman-Wunsch align user lyrics to tokens.
tasks.details: whisperx.load_model('large-v3'); transcribe(vocals.wav, word_timestamps=True); dtw_align(user_lines, whisper_words) -> [{'word':str, 'line_idx':int, 't_start':float, 't_end':float}]; save lyrics.aligned.json.
tasks.testStrategy: Input known lyrics+vocal stem, verify aligned timings match whisper output ±0.2s, preserves user text.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Decompose to: 1) Demucs vocals extraction; 2) WhisperX large-v3 transcription with word timestamps; 3) Needleman-Wunsch/DTW alignment to user lyrics and JSON output.

tasks.title: Skia Kinetic Typography Layer
tasks.description: Render typography with skia-python: per-word motions (pop-in/beat-shake etc.), lower-third layout, fonts from assets/, driven by aligned.json.
tasks.details: skia.Canvas RGBA; load preset font (TTF); for frame t: active_words = [w for w in lyrics if t in [w.t_start, w.t_end]]; animate pos/scale/alpha per preset motion curves; prev line fade.
tasks.testStrategy: Render lyrics clip, verify words appear/disappear at aligned times, motions smooth, text crisp.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) Skia Canvas setup and font loading; 2) Per-frame active words filtering from aligned.json; 3) Motion curves (pop-in/shake) implementation; 4) Lower-third layout and fade effects.

tasks.title: Frame Compositor Pipeline
tasks.description: Integrate bg + reactive + typo + logo: per-frame loop pull/interpolate, blend RGBA layers, write BGR to ffmpeg stdin.
tasks.details: pipeline/compositor.py: queue.Queue bounded; for i in range(total_frames): bg = interp_bg(i/fps); reactive=render_reactive(i); typo=render_typo(i); logo_blend; pipe bgr24 bytes to ffmpeg.proc.stdin.
tasks.testStrategy: Full render, verify layers visible/composited correctly in random frames via cv2 read.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Per-frame layer pulling/interpolation (bg/reactive/typo/logo); 2) RGBA alpha blending sequence; 3) BGR byte queue to ffmpeg stdin; 4) Threading/bounded queue for performance.

tasks.title: SDXL Background Stills Generator
tasks.description: AI stills mode: diffusers SDXL FP16, generate N=ceil(dur/8s) keyframes from sections+preset prompt, FILM interpolate/crossfade.
tasks.details: from diffusers import StableDiffusionXLPipeline; pipe=...to('cuda:0', torch.float16); prompts=[preset+section_desc]; images=pipe(prompts).images; resize 1920x1080; save cache/background/keyframe_{i}.png; interp func.
tasks.testStrategy: Generate for 30s song, verify N=4 images gen'd <60s total, interp smooth between keyframes.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Diffusers SDXL FP16 pipeline setup on CUDA; 2) Keyframe generation from sections/prompts; 3) FILM interpolation and crossfade between keyframes.

tasks.title: Background Modes Integration
tasks.description: Add AnimateDiff (stretch), static image upload/KenBurns; select mode in UI, feed to compositor.
tasks.details: If static: upload img, KenBurns zoom/pan on RMS; AnimateDiff: short loops per section (use diffusers AnimateDiffPipeline if avail); cache/background/.
tasks.testStrategy: Test each mode renders without crash, backgrounds match mode (motion/static).
tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down to: 1) Static image upload and KenBurns (zoom/pan on RMS); 2) AnimateDiff pipeline short loops; 3) UI mode selector and compositor integration.

tasks.title: Thumbnail Generator
tasks.description: Pick frame at first chorus downbeat/loudest 1s, overlay Artist—Title big text with preset typo, save 1920x1080 PNG.
tasks.details: pipeline/thumbnail.py: chorus_t = segments[1] or argmax(rms); frame = compositor.get_frame(chorus_t); skia overlay title_text; PIL save.
tasks.testStrategy: After render, verify thumbnail.png exists, shows title overlay on video frame.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Divide into: 1) Frame selection at chorus/loudest RMS; 2) Skia text overlay and PIL PNG save.

tasks.title: Metadata Generator
tasks.description: Generate metadata.txt: title, desc w/lyrics/chapters, tags from genre/preset.
tasks.details: pipeline/metadata.py: title=f'{artist} — {title} [Official Visualizer]'; chapters=[f'{t//60:02d}:{t%60:02d} {seg}' for seg,t in segments]; tags=list; write file.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify txt parses correctly, chapters match segments.
tasks.expansionPrompt: No expansion needed; basic text generation.

tasks.title: Progress, Preview 10s, Polish and Error Handling
tasks.description: Add live progress/ETA/log to Gradio; Preview 10s from loudest section; 4K/fps options; resumable renders; validate A/V sync.
tasks.details: Gradio queue+progressbar; preview: slice analysis[loudest_10s]; try/except per stage w/ resume from cache; post-render ffprobe check drift<50ms; outputs/<run_id=hash>/.
tasks.testStrategy: Full render progress updates, preview <30s, ffprobe no drift, error recovery works.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Decompose into: 1) Gradio progress/ETA/log updates; 2) 10s preview from loudest section; 3) 4K/fps options; 4) Resumable rendering from cache; 5) A/V sync validation with ffprobe.

tasks.title: Scope Preview to 10s Window with Background Optimization
tasks.description: Modify orchestrator to pass preview time range to BackgroundSource generators (SDXL, AnimateDiff, Ken Burns) for partial asset generation only within loudest 10s + padding; ensure full render completes missing assets with proper cache keys for unchanged quality.
tasks.details:
1. In orchestrator.py (from Task 5), add preview_start/end params from Task 18 loudest_10s analysis: def ensure_backgrounds(hash, preview_range=None, full_duration=None): if preview_range: t_start, t_end = preview_range; padding=1.0s; gen_range=(max(0,t_start-padding), min(full_duration, t_end+padding)) else: gen_range=(0,full_duration)
2. Update BackgroundSource.ensure(mode, song_hash, time_range=gen_range): cache_key=f'{hash}_{mode}_{gen_range[0]:.1f}_{gen_range[1]:.1f}' to distinguish partial/full caches in cache/<hash>/backgrounds/
3. Modify Task14 SDXL: pipe(prompts_for_range=[section_desc for t in np.arange(gen_range[0],gen_range[1],8)]).images → keyframe_{t:.1f}.png
4. Task15 AnimateDiff/KenBurns: generate loops/zooms only for gen_range, save with time-specific keys
5. Full render: call ensure_backgrounds(hash, full_duration=analysis['duration']) to fill gaps, using existing partial cache
6. Update docs/preview-and-render.md: explain partial caching; background-modes.md: time-range param; Gradio: 'Preview generates backgrounds for 10s window only (~2min vs 25min)'
7. Cache manifest.json: track generated ranges per mode for resume logic.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Preview 30s song: verify BackgroundSource cache keys contain 'X.X_Y.Y' (10s range), SDXL generates ~2 keyframes (<30s total), preview completes <3min
2. Full render same song: new cache keys for full range, uses partial cache (no re-gen for preview range), ffprobe full video complete/quality matches non-preview baseline
3. Check cache/<hash>/backgrounds/: partial files from preview, complete after full render; manifest.json lists ranges
4. Gradio: progress shows 'Backgrounds (10s preview)' vs 'Backgrounds (full 180s)'; docs updated verifiable
5. Edge: preview at track edge → padding clamps 0/duration; no A/V issues ffprobe drift<50ms.

tasks.title: Bass-Driven Logo Beat Pulse with Low-Frequency Energy
tasks.description: Replace generic beat pulse with bass/kick-driven animation using low-frequency energy from analysis.json; add tunable sensitivity in CompositorConfig and preserve optional 'every beat' mode.
tasks.details:
**Core Changes in compositor/beat_pulse.py:**
1. Load analysis.json: extract **low-freq energy** - use `spectrum_bands[0:2]` (bass ~20-250Hz, 8 log-mel bands from Task 4) or compute `librosa.feature.rms(y=librosa.stft(analysis['audio'], n_fft=2048)[:,:,:2])` for sub-bass.
2. **Bass envelope generator**: `def bass_envelope(analysis, frame_time, sensitivity=1.0): bass_energy = np.mean(spectrum_bands[frame_time*sr//hop:frame_time*sr//hop+frame_len, 0:2]); return np.clip(bass_energy*sensitivity, 0, 1)`
3. **Alternative signals**: kick-detect = `onset_strength[beats] > threshold` filtered to bass band, or `librosa.onset.onset_strength(analysis['audio'].lowpass(250))` peaks.
4. **Logo params**: `scale = 1.0 + 0.3*bass_envelope; opacity = 0.7 + 0.3*bass_envelope` (smooth with `scipy.signal.sosfilt` 0.1s attack/decay).
5. **Config**: In `CompositorConfig`: `pulse_mode='bass' | 'beats' | 'hybrid'; bass_sensitivity=Slider(0.1-3.0, default=1.0)`; Gradio slider in UI.
6. **Fallback**: If `pulse_mode='beats'`, use existing `beat_grid` interpolation.
7. **Docs**: Update `title-and-beat-pulse.md`: explain bass vs beats modes, sensitivity tuning, freq ranges used.
**Extend audio_analyzer.py if needed**: Add `bass_rms = librosa.rms(librosa.stft(audio)[:,:,:2])` to analysis.json.
**Cache**: `pulse_cache/{song_hash}_{mode}_{sensitivity:.1f}.npy` for envelope per frame.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. **Analysis verify**: `python -m pipeline.audio_analyzer sample_bassheavy.mp3` → check `analysis.json` has `spectrum_bands.shape[1]>=2`, `bass_rms` if added.
2. **Pulse render test**: Mock 10s analysis slice → render logo-only frames, verify **scale/opacity** peaks align with kick hits (visualize matplotlib subplot(energy, pulse)) not every hi-hat.
3. **UI test**: Gradio → Analyze bass-heavy track (e.g. EDM), slider bass_sensitivity=0.5/1.0/2.0 → Preview: logo **pulsing cleanly on kicks** (no jitter on snares), mode='beats' falls back to grid.
4. **Full render**: EDM track → ffprobe video, extract logo frames → **energy correlation**: `np.corrcoef(bass_rms[::30], logo_scales)>0.7`; weak-beat track (acoustic) → minimal/no jitter.
5. **Docs**: Verify `title-and-beat-pulse.md` explains tuning/modes.

tasks.title: Overnight / multi-song render queue
tasks.description: Allow queuing multiple full-pipeline jobs (ingest + analysis + background + compositor) so batches can run unattended, with stable file paths for uploads and clear per-job success/failure handling.
tasks.details: Define a durable job spec (serialize OrchestratorInputs or equivalent: song_hash or audio path, background mode, preset, lyrics toggle/text, resolution/fps, metadata, paths to copied logo/static assets). Prefer phased delivery: (A) CLI or script that reads JSON/JSONL and calls orchestrate_full_render sequentially; (B) optional Gradio: Add to queue, list/clear, background worker thread or subprocess so the UI stays responsive. Copy Gradio temp uploads into per-job folders when enqueueing. Between jobs, rely on existing GPU memory teardown patterns. Document in docs/technical/ or handover: queue file format, overnight usage.
tasks.testStrategy: Enqueue2+ jobs with different hashes; verify outputs land in distinct outputs/<run_id>/; failure in job 2 does not corrupt job 1 artifacts; optional: restart mid-queue resumes from remaining entries if persistence implemented.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Job spec schema + file persistence; 2) CLI batch runner calling orchestrate_full_render; 3) Stable copy of upload paths per job; 4) Optional Gradio queue UI + worker.

tasks.title: Single primary export action in Gradio (full pipeline UX)
tasks.description: Reduce perceived multi-step friction: one clear control that runs the same chain as Render full video today (analysis + optional lyrics alignment + background ensure + compositor + metadata), with advanced actions still available.
tasks.details: orchestrate_full_render already chains stages; the gap is UX/documentation. Add a prominent Export / Run full pipeline button (or rename and group) that uses the same code path as btn_render. Clarify in UI copy that Analyze / Align are optional precache/debug; when typography is enabled, render includes alignment if lyrics text present. Update docs/technical/gradio-ui.md and preview-and-render.md to describe the one-button story. Do not change stage order in orchestrator unless product requires it.
tasks.testStrategy: From cold state: ingest audio, fill metadata/preset, single export click produces output.mp4 + metadata.txt without requiring separate Analyze/Align clicks when lyrics+typography on.
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Gradio layout + button wiring mirroring full render; 2) Doc updates for user-facing flow.

tasks.title: Timestamped section headers for lyrics alignment
tasks.description: Allow the user to mark section boundaries with a single line that carries both a human-readable label (Verse 1, Chorus, Pre-chorus, Bridge, Outro, ...) AND a [m:ss] timestamp. The header produces both a section break (equivalent to today's `---`) and an immovable line anchor on the first lyric line of that section, eliminating the need to stack `---` with an inline `[m:ss]` on every section start. This becomes the ergonomic primary input channel for coarse section-level timing.
tasks.details:
Extend `pipeline/lyrics_aligner.py` parser in `_split_user_lyrics`:

1. Recognise a new header line form. Canonical examples (support all so users can paste whatever is natural):
   * `[Verse 1 0:12]`
   * `[Chorus 1:00]`
   * `[Pre-chorus 0:42.5]`
   * `[0:12 Verse 1]` (time before label)
   * `[Outro 2:55]`
   Accept either a `[m:ss]` / `[m:ss.mmm]` / `[h:mm:ss]` token inside the brackets (order-insensitive relative to the label). The header is matched on the ENTIRE trimmed line; lines that start with `[` but don't match the header grammar fall through to `_parse_anchor_prefix` (legacy bare-anchor lines) unchanged.
2. On match: emit both a section break (append to `section_starts`) AND record the timestamp in `line_anchors` keyed to the NEXT real lyric line emitted after the header. Collapse consecutive headers the same way consecutive `---` markers are collapsed.
3. Preserve the label text on the output so downstream logging and the editor can surface it (e.g. the Section-fingerprint log line can print `chosen=[Verse 1: 12.00s, Chorus: 42.50s, ...]`). This requires a small widening of the tuple returned by `_split_user_lyrics` or a sibling dict `section_labels: dict[int, str]` keyed by section index.
4. Backward compatibility: `---`, `--- <tag>`, bare `[m:ss]` prefixes on lyric lines, and bare `[m:ss]` on a standalone line must continue to work with identical semantics. Cache hashes for existing songs that use only legacy forms must not change (fold labels into `_lyrics_cache_key` canonical form only when labels/header-timestamps are actually present).
5. Wire the header timestamps into the existing `forced_sec` dict that feeds `_assign_sections_via_temporal_dp` — they should behave identically to today's inline `[m:ss]` anchors (score 2.0, dominates fingerprint match scores capped at 1.0).
6. Optional polish: surface `section_labels` in the persisted `lyrics.aligned.json` payload so the visual editor can display section badges on the waveform.
tasks.testStrategy:
Unit tests in `tests/test_lyrics_aligner.py`:
  * `TestSplitUserLyricsTimestampedHeaders` — parse each canonical header form; verify section_starts, line_anchors, and section_labels are populated; verify labels don't leak into lyric text; verify mixed documents with both `---` and timestamped headers work.
  * Backward-compat: re-run `TestSplitUserLyrics*` existing cases and assert outputs unchanged.
  * Cache-key stability: `_lyrics_cache_key` for a document using only `---` must hash identically before/after this change.
  * `TestSectionFingerprintEndToEnd` — add a case where the user provides headers on every section and verify the DP picks the header times verbatim (score 2.0 wins).
End-to-end: load one real song with 5-8 labeled section headers and confirm the `Section-fingerprint placement:` log line shows every section `chosen_starts[i]` equal to the header time (within 5 ms).
tasks.expansionPrompt: Split into: 1) Parser grammar + `_split_user_lyrics` changes with section_labels output; 2) Cache-key canonicalisation and backward-compat guard; 3) Wire into forced_sec/DP path + persist labels in lyrics.aligned.json for the editor.
